title: My Review of NEON GENESIS EVANGELION BATTLE ORCHESTRA
display_title: My Review of NEON GENESIS EVANGELION BATTLE ORCHESTRA
wikitext:
[]: My Review of NEON GENESIS EVANGELION: BATTLE ORCHESTRA             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jan 30, 2008 3:19 pm Now that I have a Playstation 2, Swap Magic and Magic Switch, I am able to play Region 1 PS2 games, including Neon Genesis Evangelion: Battle Orchestra . You may have seen some gameplay videos at the game's official site . It looks a lot like Super Smash Bros. , doesn't it? Indeed it is! The main difference in gameplay between SSB and NGE:BO is that Battle Orchestra is HP-based instead of knock-the-character-off-the-screen-based (though SSB actually does have an option to play in HP-mode). Even SSB-style crates full of items occasionally appear with NGE-themed weapons and health-restoring items. Despite the fact that the game is a SSB clone, it is still quite fun. A lot of it is in English, so it's easy to navigate through the game. You start out able to play as Unit 01, Unit 00, or Unit 02, though you can probably unlock Kensuke and possibly Kaworu, considering the opening. You can play Story Mode on easy, medium, or hard. You face different enemies depending on which Evangelion you choose. Sometimes you have one or both of the other Evas assist you in battle. I beat easy modes for Units 01 and 00, but Unit 02's final battle is against three MP Evangelions at once... and once you beat them, they respawn with all their health . I haven't been able to figure out how to win that stage. Perhaps you just have to survive until the timer runs out, but that usually results in you losing. Now, this game absolutely does not take itself seriously, and that's part of what makes it fun. Leliel throws punches by jutting parts of itself outward. Sahaquiel is now Evangelion-size and can do a barrel roll to attack. One of the items you can use is PenPen, which you throw at your enemy to inflict minor damage. The list goes on. One of the levels is the Nerv HQ pyramid. I do wonder if Broccoli knew if it was supposed to be a vagina. If they did know, it's even funnier. The concave pyramid is filled up with water (representing an overactive Bartholin's gland?) and a not-nearly-to-scale PenPen is floating in it, watching your battle. There are also floating platforms, and there is another one of those large, flat skyscrapers on the other side of the pyramid as well, meaning this particular vagina has two clitoral hoods , one of which is nowhere remotely near the clitoris. You earn points by winning, which you can then use to shop for things like crystal meth... erm, additional BGM. As I die a lot, I don't usually end up with many points and haven't unlocked much yet. That's about it. I recorded the opening intro, so I'll probably upload that for those of you who wish to see it. I'll also probably add some stuff to the wiki's entry for the game. I bet the wiki doesn't even have an entry for this game yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 30, 2008 3:47 pm Naisu, NAveryW! By the way, do you mean Region 2? And I don't think the Japanese games really have regions, but... Anyway, now I'm sort of interested in playing it, too. The only thing is, something about the artstyle bothers me...Not sure why or what it is. Oh, and the MP Eva Series also happen to restore their health (all 9 of em) in the Evangelion2 game, like in the film, which of course makes it REALLY hard to beat. -_-

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Jan 30, 2008 4:51 pm I think I saw some people playing this at Otakon '07. It looked like fun, though I didn't get a chance to play it myself.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jan 30, 2008 8:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: By the way, do you mean Region 2?

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Wed Jan 30, 2008 11:10 pm Do you know who pilots the Green Evangelion? I know Kaworu pilots the golden one, but...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 31, 2008 6:59 pm I'm pretty sure the greenie is piloted by Kensuke.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Jan 31, 2008 7:04 pm I've really been wanting this game, hearing about all of the features and stuff included makes me want it even more... Swap Magic and Magic Keys does allow me to play burned games, this makes it very tempting to start scouring torrent sites later... hm...

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Thu Jan 31, 2008 8:25 pm NAveryW wrote: I'm pretty sure the greenie is piloted by Kensuke.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 31, 2008 9:05 pm Then Toji must be the remaining pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 31, 2008 9:47 pm http://www.mediafire.com/?1kjwzf4m5xa Behold the high quality intro recorded at its native resolution!

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Thu Jan 31, 2008 11:32 pm NAveryW wrote: Then Toji must be the remaining pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by ungenesis » Tue Feb 12, 2008 2:27 pm So, any more info to offer on this game? How are the 'clone' characters (Beserk Eva-01, Eva-00', Bardiel, etc) and the 'new' Evas (Alpha and Beta?)?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Feb 12, 2008 4:04 pm ungenesis wrote: So, any more info to offer on this game? How are the 'clone' characters (Beserk Eva-01, Eva-00', Bardiel, etc) and the 'new' Evas (Alpha and Beta?)?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 12, 2008 5:33 pm *Me cries ala Rod, or Todd. Whichever one it was*

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Feb 12, 2008 5:39 pm /is also curly-haired

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Fri Feb 15, 2008 4:34 pm I bought a I Pirate version of the game, the Green Eva(Eva Unit Beta) is apparently controled by the Dummyplug. there also an unlockable Gunbuster. Dont know about the story, but what I DO know is that if you play as Zeruel, Just hitting Foward and SQUARE, he does this double slap thingamajig, wich is REALY cheap, and makes the game easy even on Hard setting. PS: Can anybosy tell me how to get Rei to love me in Ayanami Ikusei Keikaku And Asuka Hokan Keikaku PS2? Plus how to make her remember me? THNX n' ADVNCE!

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Sat Feb 16, 2008 7:18 pm soljerameelguy wrote: I bought a I Pirate version of the game, the Green Eva(Eva Unit Beta) is apparently controled by the Dummyplug.
